2606.03939.title: FlashbackCL: Mitigating Temporal Forgetting in Federated Learning
2606.03939.paper_summary: Federated Learning (FL) of foundation and edge models increasingly targets deployments where client data distributions drift over time, yet existing forgetting-mitigation methods assume each client's distribution is stationary. Flashback, the strongest recent FL method against cross-client (spatial) forgetting, uses monotonically accumulating per-class label counts as a knowledge proxy; this proxy becomes miscalibrated under temporal distribution shift and anchors the global model to an outdated class balance. We formalise temporal forgetting in FL with a per-phase metric isolated from protocol-level fluctuations and propose Flashback Continual Learning (FlashbackCL), a drop-in extension of Flashback with (i) temporally-decayed label counts; (ii) a device-aware replay buffer with Class-Balanced Reservoir Sampling (CBRS); and (iii) server-side active coreset curation on the public distillation set. The results show that FlashbackCL achieves 6.9% to 10.0% relative improvement relative to Flashback, on CIFAR-10 with 50 clients and three controlled temporal shift modes, while simultaneously reducing temporal forgetting by up to 68%. A 5-variant ablation identifies CBRS replay as the critical component. FlashbackCL also improves Flashback by 3.5 points on stationary CIFAR-100, suggesting that class-balanced replay regularises spatial heterogeneity as well as temporal shift.
2606.03939.paper_summary_zh: 聯邦學習（FL）對基礎和邊緣模型的應用越來越多地針對客戶端數據分佈隨時間漂移的情況，然而現有的遺忘緩解方法假設每個客戶端的分佈是靜態的。Flashback，最近對抗跨客戶端（空間）遺忘的最強FL方法，使用每類標籤計數的單調累積作為知識代理；這個代理在時間分佈變化下會失去校準，並將全局模型固定在過時的類別平衡上。我們用一個與協議層級波動隔離的每階段指標來形式化FL中的時間遺忘，並提出Flashback持續學習（FlashbackCL），這是一個Flashback的即插即用擴展，具有（i）時間衰減的標籤計數；（ii）具有類別平衡的水庫抽樣（CBRS）的設備感知重放緩衝區；以及（iii）在公共蒸餾集上的伺服器端主動核心集策劃。結果顯示，FlashbackCL在CIFAR-10上對50個客戶端和三種控制的時間漂移模式，相對於Flashback實現了6.9%到10.0%的相對改善，同時將時間遺忘減少了高達68%。五個變體的消融實驗確定CBRS重放是關鍵組件。FlashbackCL還在靜態CIFAR-100上將Flashback的表現提高了3.5個點，這表明類別平衡的重放不僅正則化了空間異質性，還正則化了時間漂移。
2606.03939.author: Mubarak A. Ojewale et.al.
2606.03939.authors: Mubarak A. Ojewale, Adriana E. Chis, Jorge M. Cortes-Mendoza, Bernardo Pulido-Gaytan, Horacio Gonzalez-Velez
